Title: New from Microsoft’s 8080 Books imprint: ‘Hyderabad Days: The code we lived by before we coded’
Author: stclarke
EnhancedContent:
Ravi Vedula’s childhood memoir of the colorful, chaotic Hyderabad of the 1980s and 1990s delivers more than vivid descriptions of local cricket heroes or an era of shared televisions.

“[Hyderabad Days](https://unlocked.microsoft.com/8080-books/#modal-book-05),” recently released by 8080 Books, pays homage to resiliency, community and values. Vedula, corporate vice president of engineering for Microsoft’s IDEAS team, describes how the scramble for a few rupees for a cricket ball became a penchant for innovation. Childhood disputes fostered diplomatic skills. Friendships defied class. It is the story of how those joy-soaked years forged the instincts of an engineer and, later, the perspective of a leader.

Microsoft’s imprint, [8080 Books](https://unlocked.microsoft.com/8080-books/), works with authors to explore how technology is shaping society, work and human experience. All revenue is donated to nonprofit organizations through Microsoft Philanthropies.
Description: The post [New from Microsoft’s 8080 Books imprint: ‘Hyderabad Days: The code we lived by before we coded’](https://news.microsoft.com/signal/articles/the-code-they-lived-by-before-they-coded/) appeared first on [Source](https://news.microsoft.com/source).
